name: Cañón de Casarejos y Cañón del río Lobos – circular desde Casarejos por el barranco de la Calzada
region_intro: Hiking around Casarejos offers access to the Cañón del Río Lobos Natural Park, a region characterized by deep fluvial canyons carved into limestone by the River Lobos. The landscape features extensive pine and holm oak forests, with dramatic rock formations and cliffs. This area in Soria, Spain, provides diverse terrain for outdoor activities, including unique geological phenomena like "simas" (sinkholes). Best hiking trails around Casarejos The most popular hiking route is Cañón de Casarejos y Cañón del río Lobos – circular desde Casarejos por el barranco de la Calzada, an 9.0 miles (14.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 30 minutes to complete, exploring the natural park's canyons. Another top favourite among local hikers is Colmenar de los Frailes – Boca del Ocejo loop from Parque Natural del Cañón del Río Lobos, a moderate 11.1 miles (17.9 km) path, offering varied terrain within the natural park. Local hikers also love the Bridge of the Seven Eyes – Boca del Ocejo loop from Parque Natural del Cañón del Río Lobos, a 4.0 miles (6.5 km) trail leading through the natural park with views of the river, often completed in about 1 hour 44 minutes. Hiking around Casarejos is defined by dramatic canyons, flowing rivers, and extensive pine and holm oak forests. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Casarejos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 190 reviews. More than 1500 hikers have used komoot to explore Casarejos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find around Casarejos?
A: Casarejos offers a diverse network of over 35 hiking trails, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a mix of easy, moderate, and difficult routes, with 13 easy, 18 moderate, and 6 difficult options available. The landscape is characterized by dramatic canyons, flowing rivers, and extensive pine and holm oak forests, particularly within the Cañón del Río Lobos Natural Park.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes near Casarejos?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Casarejos are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular moderate circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1062355718/canon-de-casarejos-y-canon-del-rio-lobos-circular-desde-casarejos-por-el-barranco-de-la-calzada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañón de Casarejos y Cañón del río Lobos – circular desde Casarejos por el barranco de la Calzada&lt;/a&gt;, which covers about 14.4 km (9 miles) and explores the natural park's canyons.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while hiking in Casarejos?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty, primarily centered around the Cañón del Río Lobos Natural Park. You can explore impressive canyons carved by the Lobos River, unique geological formations, and extensive pine and holm oak forests. Don't miss the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3634900" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gullurías Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic views of the Soria and Burgos pine forests and the Iberian System peaks. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1072363" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva Grande (Río Lobos Canyon)&lt;/a&gt;, a significant cave within the park.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the trails?
A: Absolutely. A prominent historical landmark is the 13th-century &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1072360" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Saint Bartholomew&lt;/a&gt;, nestled within the Cañón del Río Lobos. This former Templar monastery, with its transitional Romanesque to Gothic architecture, offers a serene and historically rich stop. The village of Casarejos itself also showcases traditional architecture with masonry, exposed brick, and wooden balconies.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Casarejos suitable for families?
A: Yes, Casarejos offers several easy trails that are suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17543491" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge of the Seven Eyes – Boca del Ocejo loop from Parque Natural del Cañón del Río Lobos&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.4 km (4 miles) route, perfect for a shorter family outing to enjoy the natural park's scenery.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Casarejos?
A: Many trails in the Casarejos area, particularly within the Cañón del Río Lobos Natural Park, are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in natural parks, to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Be sure to check specific park regulations or trail signs for any restrictions.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Casarejos?
A: The best time to hike in Casarejos is typically during spring, summer, and early autumn. These seasons offer pleasant weather for exploring the canyons and forests. Spring brings blooming water lilies and dragonflies around the Hermitage of San Bartolomé, while autumn showcases beautiful fall foliage in the pine and holm oak forests.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Casarejos?
A: The hiking routes in Casarejos are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an impressive average rating of 4.9 stars from over 200 reviews. Hikers often praise the dramatic canyon landscapes, the tranquility of the pine and holm oak forests, and the well-maintained paths that offer a truly immersive outdoor experience.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging hikes available?
A: For those seeking a greater challenge, Casarejos has several difficult routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31846202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente de los Agualinos – Footbridge over the Río Lobos loop from San Leonardo de Yagüe&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 22.8 km (14.2 miles) trail that takes you through varied terrain and offers extensive exploration of the natural park.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads?
A: While specific parking details for every trailhead are not always listed, Casarejos, being a village at the heart of a natural park, generally offers parking options near popular access points to the Cañón del Río Lobos. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival or consult detailed route descriptions on komoot for specific parking recommendations for your chosen trail.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see while hiking?
A: The Cañón del Río Lobos Natural Park is home to diverse wildlife. Keep an eye out for various bird species, including raptors soaring above the canyons. The forests are also home to mammals such as deer and wild boar, though they are often shy. The river areas, especially around the Hermitage of San Bartolomé, are known for water lilies and dragonflies.
tips: Beautiful canyon that goes from Casarejos to the popular Cañón del Río Lobos. During our walk, we will find caves and beautiful trails.

Cueva Grande is a natural cave located in the Cañón del Río Lobos natural park, specifically in the area of the Templar hermitage of San Bartolomé. It has numerous legends and has also been used to film numerous reports and films. It is an idyllic place full of peace.

Located in a unique setting, isolated at the mouth of the Lobos River Canyon, this beautiful construction was part of a Templar monastery, San Juan de Otero, of which only the chapel remains. The architectural symbols inscribed on its walls have been the source of numerous debates, attributing some to them of esoteric origin.

waypoints.name: Entrance Trail to Cañón del Río Lobos from Casarejos (GR-86)
